title: QUIC RFC 9001 1-RTT Key Update Lifecycle Work Item
summary: Plan the trace-safe widening from the current first-successor Key Phase floor to a repeated 1-RTT key-update lifecycle, including old-key retention and discard, without treating the existing first-update helper as full RFC 9001 support.
planned_changes:
- Introduce a dedicated connection-owned 1-RTT key-update lifecycle owner with current, next, and old packet-protection epoch state.
- Preserve the existing first-successor install path as a bounded prerequisite rather than widening its single-update guard in place.
- Add repeated local-initiated and peer-initiated update handling that alternates the Key Phase bit, derives successor write/read material from the current epoch, and rejects duplicate, stale, tampered, packet-number-incoherent, or unauthenticated transitions without advancing state.
- Add current-phase acknowledgment gating for repeated local updates and feed AEAD usage-limit update requests through that same gate.
- Add old 1-RTT key retention and discard rules tied to packet number, reordering, three-PTO retention, AEAD usage, and sender/recovery cleanup instead of helper-only secret deletion.
- Keep TLS KeyUpdate prohibition, post-handshake ticket ingestion, 0-RTT, public API promises, and interop expansion out of this RFC 9001 lifecycle slice unless separately traced.
verification_plan: The executable slice must include positive tests for repeated local and peer updates, negative tests for duplicate/stale/tampered transitions, current-phase acknowledgment gating, packet-number-incoherent old-key packets, AEAD-limit-driven update requests, and premature discard, fuzz or property coverage for Key Phase observation and epoch transition boundaries, and BenchmarkDotNet coverage for the hot successor-derivation/install/open path. The current first-update tests and QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks remain baseline evidence only.
completion_notes: Planned for the broader lifecycle. The direct first-successor proof for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0010 is now executable at the requirement-home level, including retained old first-update 1-RTT packet-protection material before and after new-key packet authentication plus explicit retained cleanup. The first peer-initiated update proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0002 for opening next-key packets only when they carry the next Key Phase bit and updating send keys before ACK emission. The first next-receive-key retention proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0002 by retaining current and next receive material for apparent peer updates and clearing the retained next material once the first successor is installed. The first send-key ordering proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0001 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0002 for first-update ACK sends and locally built higher-packet-number packets. The first old/current next receive-selection proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0003, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0001, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0002 for rejecting old-key packets that violate the first successor packet-number floor, using retained old receive material when recovered packet numbers fall below the current phase floor, and using next receive material when recovered packet numbers rise above that floor. No repeated 1-RTT key-update implementation, unconfirmed consecutive peer-update error policy, old-key ACK error policy, receive-key selection beyond the first successor, AEAD-limit update trigger, or old-key discard implementation is claimed by this artifact.

supplemental_sections.heading: Current Boundary
supplemental_sections.content: The current runtime can prove the first 0-to-1 successor install, successor write-key use, stable header-protection keys, updated-key protection for subsequent packets, pre-confirmation local-update rejection, bounded repeat-update rejection before any current-phase acknowledgment support exists, successor receive-key installation, retained next first-update receive material, retained old first-update 1-RTT material before and after new-key packet authentication, explicit retained cleanup, first peer-initiated next-key packet opening, updated send-key ACK protection for peer-initiated updates, first-update send-key ordering, the first old/current next receive-selection floors for lower and higher recovered packet numbers, the first KEY_UPDATE_ERROR floor for old-key packet-number ordering violations, QUIC `quic ku` successor traffic-secret derivation, and packet key/IV derivation from updated secrets. Repeated key-update cycles after acknowledgment, unconfirmed consecutive peer-update error policy, old-key ACK error policy, receive-key selection across retained/current/next epochs beyond the first successor, AEAD-limit update requests, and old 1-RTT key discard remain planned until the lifecycle owner is implemented and proven.
